claim_set_title: BCMA Antigen Escape Claim Set v0

claims.claim_text: BCMA target presence, absence, assay type, specimen context, and prior BCMA-directed exposure should be extracted before public maps interpret sequential anti-BCMA strategies.
supports: Adding target-status fields to post-BCMA relapse extraction records.
Preserving prior BCMA-directed exposure and target-verification context in sequential therapy maps.
does_not_support: Using soluble BCMA or marrow BCMA status as a standalone patient-specific rule.
limitations: The v0 public claim is built from extracted public abstract and metadata fields.
Assay method, timing, specimen source, and full clinical context need expert review.

claims.claim_text: TNFRSF17 alteration or loss is a source-supported BCMA antigen escape signal to track, especially when paired pre-treatment and relapse samples are available.
supports: Tracking TNFRSF17 status as a target-alteration field in BCMA relapse maps.
Separating target-alteration evidence from broader low-antigen-density observations.
does_not_support: Estimating TNFRSF17-loss frequency across all BCMA-directed products or disease states.
Treating one target-alteration signal as proof that antigen escape explains every relapse.
limitations: The source extraction records a small mechanism-positive subset and one genetic observation from the public abstract.
Frequency, assay reproducibility, and product-specific context require fuller review.

claims.claim_text: Low-to-negative BCMA expression after BCMA CAR T relapse supports tracking alternate-target research signals, including SEMA4A in the cited translational and preclinical source.
supports: Creating alternate-target research fields that preserve target density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, and clinical translation status.
Tracking SEMA4A as a candidate alternate-target signal in post-BCMA relapse maps.
does_not_support: Claiming SEMA4A-directed CAR T therapy works in patients.
Recommending a SEMA4A-directed trial or any alternate-target intervention.
limitations: The public extraction is translational and preclinical.
Target expression and preclinical activity are not the same as clinical efficacy or safety.

claims.claim_text: BCMA antigen escape should not be treated as the only relapse mechanism; public maps should continue to track tumor-intrinsic, immune-cell, microenvironment, sequencing, disease-context, and measurement factors.
supports: Keeping the post-CAR T relapse map multi-bucket rather than reducing relapse to antigen loss alone.
Preserving immune profiling, genomic profiling, sequencing, persistence, exhaustion, microenvironment, and measurement fields for later review.
does_not_support: Proving any resistance-overcoming strategy improves survival.
Ranking BCMA-directed CAR T-cell therapy, bispecific antibodies, or other intervention classes.
limitations: Review framing and observational immune-state signals require source-by-source extraction before scoring.
Mechanism categories may overlap and can be confounded by product, disease state, prior therapy, and measurement timing.

review_notes: Promoted from four public extraction records under post-car-t-relapse-mechanism-map-v0.
Claim set is a public evidence-organization artifact, not a clinical summary or treatment guide.
